2606.11918.title: The Art of Interrogation: Consistency Amplifies Factuality in Spatial Reasoning
2606.11918.paper_summary: Current Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) exhibit remarkable general capabilities but significantly underperform in spatial reasoning tasks. Existing approaches treat this gap as a knowledge deficit, relying on supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to ingest labeled spatial data from external vision sources or synthetic engines. In contrast, we argue that for many tasks, spatial reasoning capabilities are already present in pre-trained LRMs but require alignment through logical coherence under geometric 2D and 3D constraints. In this work, we propose a self-supervised reinforcement learning (RL) framework that targets the internal reasoning process without requiring ground-truth annotations. By formalizing the notion of consistency verifiers -- reward functions that check for geometric and semantic consistency under transformations -- we demonstrate that models can improve their spatial reasoning abilities. We use both image transformations, like flipping, and textual transformations, like swapping the order of objects in the question, and propose a new optimal transport-based RL strategy, OT-GRPO, which is a minimal-matching variant of group relative policy optimization tailored to pairwise verifiers. We show that this label-free consistency training approaches the accuracy of models trained with ground-truth supervision and achieves similar generalization across diverse tasks and data domains.
2606.11918.paper_summary_zh: 目前的大型推理模型（LRMs）展現出卓越的通用能力，但在空間推理任務中表現顯著不足。現有的方法將這一差距視為知識缺失，依賴於監督式微調（SFT）來從外部視覺來源或合成引擎中攝取標記的空間數據。相反，我們主張對於許多任務，空間推理能力已經存在於預訓練的LRMs中，但需要通過幾何2D和3D約束下的邏輯一致性來進行對齊。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個自我監督的強化學習（RL）框架，旨在針對內部推理過程，而不需要真實標註。通過形式化一致性驗證器的概念——檢查在變換下幾何和語義一致性的獎勵函數——我們展示了模型可以提高其空間推理能力。我們使用了圖像變換，如翻轉，以及文本變換，如在問題中交換物體的順序，並提出了一種基於最優運輸的RL策略OT-GRPO，這是一種針對成對驗證器的最小匹配變體的群體相對政策優化。我們顯示這種無標籤的一致性訓練接近於使用真實監督訓練的模型的準確性，並在不同任務和數據領域中達到了類似的泛化能力。
2606.11918.author: Theo Uscidda et.al.
2606.11918.authors: Theo Uscidda, Marta Tintore Gazulla, Maks Ovsjanikov, Federico Tombari, Leonidas Guibas
